SEARCH
Package mvc.model

Class Model

java.lang.Object
mvc.model.Model
public class Model extends Object
Represents the Model component in the MVC pattern. Its primary task is the implementation of the homophonic substitution cipher. This includes: - Homophonic key generation. Key is generated based on customizable parameters such as single phonogram length, maximal and minimal number of phonograms per letter. - Encoding of strings. - Decoding of strings according to generated key as well as the default key. Encoding characters (phonograms) length and number assinged to letters can be customized, however combination of these values must meet specific requirements so that a valid key can be generated.
Version:
1.0
Author:
Milosz Liniewiecki

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Model()
    Constructs the Model instances and initilizes internal parameters: allEncodedCharacters, allPhonograms, key, frequencyTable, nonHandledCharacters.

  • Constructor Details

    • Model

      public Model()
      Constructs the Model instances and initilizes internal parameters: allEncodedCharacters, allPhonograms, key, frequencyTable, nonHandledCharacters. The frequency table is initilized with letters from the english alphabet.

  • Method Details

    • init

      public void init(int phonogramLength, int maxPhonograms, int minPhonograms, boolean useDefault)
      Initilizes fields related to key generation and generates the key (or uses default one). Clears the key before generating it.
      Parameters:
      phonogramLength - value assinged to phonogramLength
      maxPhonograms - value assinged to maxPhonograms
      minPhonograms - value assinged to minPhonograms.
      useDefault - if true, default key is used.

    • encrypt

      public String encrypt(String inputString)
      Accepts a message string and encrypts it according to key. Trailing and preceding whitespaces are removed if they are present. Whitespaces are not encoded. Function iterates over all letters from the input string and assignes each one a randomly selected phonogram according to the key.
      Parameters:
      inputString - string that is to be encrypted.
      Returns:
      Encrypted string.

    • decrypt

      public String decrypt(String inputString)
      Accepts a message string, composed of phonograms and decodes it to english alphabet. Removes leading and trailing whitespaces. Whitespaces are not decoded, they remain unchanged. Function iterates over phongrams, phonogramLength specify single phonogram length. Each phonogram is mapped to letter from english alphabet present in the key.
      Parameters:
      inputString - encoded string should contain only digits and spaces.
      Returns:
      input string decrypted to english alphabet.

    • testEncryptedString

      public boolean testEncryptedString(String inputString) throws UnknownPhonogramException, StringIndexOutOfBoundsException
      Validates encrypted string, if it contains legal phonogram characters (phonogram must belong to allPhonograms. Leading and trailing whitespaces are removed. Spaces that take more than one space are normalised. Splits the given string on whitespaces and iterates over specific words. Extracts phonograms of length phonogramLength and checks if allPhonograms contains it.
      Parameters:
      inputString - Encoded string that we want to decode.
      Returns:
      true if the string is validand contains only known phongrams. Otherwise exception is thrown.
      Throws:
      UnknownPhonogramException - in case an unknown phonogram has been found in the string.
      StringIndexOutOfBoundsException

    • getTotalPhonogramsNum

      public int getTotalPhonogramsNum()
      Returns number of all phonograms.
      Returns:
      value of totalPhonogramsNum.

    • getNonHandledCharacters

      public Pattern getNonHandledCharacters()
      Returns the pattern, which specifies the illegal characters.
      Returns:
      pattern nonHandledCharacters.

    • getAllEncodedCharacters

      public String[] getAllEncodedCharacters()
      Returns the array of valid characters that can be encoded.
      Returns:
      valid characters from allEncodedCharacters.

    • getAllPhonograms

      public String[] getAllPhonograms()
      Returns all encryption characters.
      Returns:
      encryption characters from allPhonograms.

    • suggestedNumberOfPhonograms

      public int suggestedNumberOfPhonograms(int suggestedMin, int suggestedMax)
      Calculates the nuber of phongrams that will be needed to encode the whole alphabet. Iterates over the frequency table frequencyTable and calculates number of phongrams that will be generated for each letter. Sum of these values is returned.
      Parameters:
      suggestedMin - minimal number of phonograms per letter.
      suggestedMax - maximal number of phongrams per letter.
      Returns:
      total number of phonograms that will be generated for the alphabet.
      See Also:
      • isPhonogramsNumSufficient(int,int,int)

    • isPhonogramsNumSufficient

      public boolean isPhonogramsNumSufficient(int suggestedMin, int suggestedMax, int suggestedLength)
      Checks if provided combination of minimal and maximal numbers of phongrams, assigned to a single letter as well as the length of a phonogram are valid. Total number of generated phonogram characters is equal 10^N, where N = phonogramLength. It must exceed value returned by suggestedNumberOfPhonograms(int, int). If so, the combination of parameters is considered valid.
      Parameters:
      suggestedMin - suggested value for minPhonograms.
      suggestedMax - suggested value for maxPhonograms.
      suggestedLength - suggested value for phonogramLength.
      Returns:
      true in case of valid parameter combination.

    • requestKey

      public Map<String,String[]> requestKey()
      Returns the main homophony encoding table. Encoded letter (String) is mapped to an array of encoding characters (String[]).
      Returns:
      homophony map key.
